technology: Outbound status email provider path for BACKLOG-015
source: John PWA instruction 2026-05-27T19:20Z; Resend send-email API docs fetched 2026-05-27T20:41Z; live SMTP credential check 2026-05-27T20:40Z
summary: Choose a non-Google transactional email API path for CTO status email instead of Gmail OAuth or the rejected Gmail App Password/SMTP path.

community_evidence.production_deployments: Transactional email APIs are a standard production pattern; this tick verified the selected Resend endpoint shape against vendor docs rather than community sentiment.
community_evidence.practitioner_sentiment: Not surveyed in this short pump tick because John explicitly requested a provider-path decision and implementation, not a broad email-provider research pass.

evaluation.what_it_makes_obsolete: The rejected Gmail App Password/SMTP path for BACKLOG-015 and any need to couple routine CTO reporting to Google's user OAuth flow.
evaluation.cost_of_switching: Low code cost: status email sender can choose an HTTP API provider via env names only. Runtime credential provisioning remains separate and secret-managed.
evaluation.cost_of_staying: BACKLOG-015 remains indefinitely blocked on invalid Gmail SMTP credentials and no regular email report channel to John.

rationale: [verified] John rejected the Gmail App Password/SMTP path and explicitly instructed CTO to pick a path for BACKLOG-015. [verified] The live configured SMTP credentials currently fail authentication with Gmail 535, without printing secret values. [verified] Resend's send-email API accepts from/to/subject/text fields and Authorization bearer API keys, which fits CTO's env-only secret boundary. [unverified] Resend is the best long-term provider; it is the chosen low-blast-radius implementation target until John provides or approves a different non-Google provider credential.

test_results.notes: python3 -m unittest -v tests/test_send_status_email.py passed. No email was sent. Existing SMTP --check-credentials failed with Gmail 535 and was treated as evidence the old path is not viable.

rollback.instructions: Unset CTO_EMAIL_PROVIDER/CTO_EMAIL_API_KEY and the sender falls back to legacy SMTP behavior; revert scripts/send-status-email.py and tests/test_send_status_email.py if necessary.
